questions.question: What is the keyboard shortcut used to access the Scheduling panel in Notion Calendar?

options.text: Ctrl + S
options.explanation: Ctrl + S is the standard keyboard shortcut for saving content in most applications, but Notion Calendar uses 'S' for Scheduling.

options.text: S
options.explanation: The keyboard shortcut 'S' is used to quickly access the Scheduling panel in the left context panel.

options.text: Alt + S
options.explanation: Alt + S is not a shortcut for Scheduling; it is used for system-specific commands in various operating systems.

options.text: Cmd + S
options.explanation: Cmd + S is the standard keyboard shortcut for saving documents on Mac, but not for opening the Scheduling panel in Notion Calendar.

questions.question: When should you use a recurring scheduling link instead of a one-off link?

options.text: When you need to meet a specific person at a specific time only once.
options.explanation: One-off links are designed for specific person/time meetings, whereas recurring links are better for interviews, sales, or meetings happening often with different people.

options.text: When the meeting requires video conferencing.
options.explanation: Both recurring and one-off links support video conferencing links; the choice depends on the frequency and repetition of the meeting.

options.text: When you want to set a minimum time limit for bookings.
options.explanation: While both link types support booking windows with minimum and maximum time limits, the distinction between them is frequency, not just the time settings.

options.text: When you are conducting user interviews or sales calls with different people regularly.
options.explanation: Recurring links are ideal for activities that happen often but involve different people, such as user interviews or sales calls.

questions.question: How do you remove an entire day's availability from a recurring scheduling link?

options.text: Click the ••• next to the day and select Delete.
options.explanation: Delete is used to remove a specific time range within a day for a one-off link, not an entire day for a recurring link.

options.text: Select the day and press the Backspace key.
options.explanation: There is no direct keyboard shortcut provided in the article to delete an entire day's availability.

options.text: Click the ••• next to the day and select Clear day.
options.explanation: To remove an entire day's availability for a recurring link, you click the ••• menu and select 'Clear day'.

options.text: Drag the day to the trash icon in the sidebar.
options.explanation: The article does not mention dragging days to a trash icon; the specific action is via the ••• menu.

questions.question: Under which specific condition is the ability to reschedule or cancel a meeting unavailable for a one-off link?

options.text: When the 'Avoid conflicts' toggle is turned off.
options.explanation: The 'Avoid conflicts' toggle affects how availability is updated based on new bookings, but it does not disable the rescheduling feature itself.

options.text: When a video conferencing link has not been added.
options.explanation: Video conferencing links are optional add-ons and do not restrict the rescheduling functionality.

options.text: When the 'Single-use link' option is enabled.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that 'Rescheduling is unavailable if Single-use link is enabled for a one-off link.'

options.text: When the meeting description is left blank.
options.explanation: The meeting description can be customized, but an empty description does not prevent rescheduling.

questions.question: How can you quickly copy the URL of an existing scheduling link?

options.text: Click the link directly in the list.
options.explanation: Clicking the link directly usually navigates to the scheduling page or opens the URL in a new tab; it does not copy the URL to the clipboard.

options.text: Select ••• and choose 'Copy scheduling link'.
options.explanation: Selecting the ••• menu next to a scheduling link and choosing 'Copy scheduling link' is the correct method to copy the URL for sharing.

options.text: Right-click the link and select 'Copy'.
options.explanation: While right-clicking might work on a web browser, the article specifically instructs to use the ••• menu within the Notion interface.

options.text: Select the 🔗 icon next to the link.
options.explanation: The 🔗 icon is used to copy the link for easy sharing, but the article specifically mentions using the ••• menu for copying existing links.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as an optional feature you can add to a scheduling link?

options.text: Meeting notes
options.explanation: The article lists optional features as expiration dates, locations, video conferencing links, phone numbers, booking windows, and descriptions. 'Meeting notes' is not mentioned.

options.text: Expiration date
options.explanation: You can add an expiration date to a scheduling link.

options.text: Video conferencing links
options.explanation: Video conferencing links are listed as an optional feature.

options.text: Phone numbers
options.explanation: Phone numbers are listed as an optional feature.

questions.question: How does the 'Avoid conflicts' feature behave regarding the use of a single scheduling link for multiple bookings?

options.text: It stops working automatically after the first booking is made.
options.explanation: The feature does not stop working; it updates your availability based on new conflicts.

options.text: It automatically cancels any new conflicts that arise.
options.explanation: The feature updates availability rather than cancelling events.

options.text: It updates availability based on new conflicts and continues working as long as some time remains available.
options.explanation: As long as some of your calendar holds remain available, you can use the same scheduling link for multiple bookings.

options.text: It generates a new unique link for every conflict that arises.
options.explanation: The feature does not generate new links; it simply updates the existing link's availability.

questions.question: For a one-off scheduling link, how can you define your available times?

options.text: Enter times manually for each day.
options.explanation: Manual entry is the method for recurring links (entering times for each day), not one-off links.

options.text: Drag on the calendar to create availability.
options.explanation: For one-off links, you can drag on the calendar to create availability, or simply enter the time.

options.text: Select preset time blocks from a dropdown menu.
options.explanation: The article does not mention preset time blocks; you must either drag or enter the time.

options.text: Upload an iCal file to set availability.
options.explanation: Uploading an iCal file is not mentioned as a method for setting availability on a scheduling link.

questions.question: Why is a new reschedule or cancel link generated in the event description each time it is used?

options.text: To ensure the link is always fresh and active.
options.explanation: While this might seem true, the article cites a specific reason: security.

options.text: To track user activity and usage history.
options.explanation: The article does not state that this is for tracking history.

options.text: For security reasons.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that 'For security reasons, every time a link to reschedule or cancel a meeting is used, a new link will be generated.'

options.text: To prevent spam or bot attacks on the calendar.
options.explanation: While security is the goal, the specific reason given in the text is for security purposes regarding the link itself.

questions.question: If a user has scheduled time with you using your scheduling link, what requirement must be met for them to see the option to reschedule or cancel the meeting?

options.text: The booking window must be less than 1 hour.
options.explanation: The booking window settings (min/max time) do not determine if rescheduling is available.

options.text: Multiple bookings must be allowed for your scheduling link.
options.explanation: The article notes that rescheduling options are available if you have 'allowed multiple bookings when sharing your availability'.

options.text: The event must be at least 24 hours away.
options.explanation: There is no time restriction mentioned for when rescheduling becomes available.

options.text: The description must include a reason for the change.
options.explanation: Providing a reason is an *option* after clicking reschedule/cancel, not a requirement to see the link itself.
